question: The initial program that is run when the computer is powered up is called __________
How does the software trigger an interrupt?

options.a: Sending signals to CPU through bus
options.b: Executing a special operation called system call
options.c: Executing a special program called system program
options.d: Executing a special program called interrupt trigger program

options.a: hardware generated interrupt caused by an error
options.b: software generated interrupt caused by an error
options.c: user generated interrupt caused by an error

options.a: It is an address that is indexed to an interrupt handler
options.b: It is a unique device number that is indexed by an address
options.c: It is a unique identity given to an interrupt

options.a: High speed devices(disks and communications network)
the CPU uses polling to watch the control bit constantly, looping to see if a device is ready
options.b: the CPU writes one data byte to the data register and sets a bit in control register to show that a byte is available
options.c: the CPU receives an interrupt when the device is ready for the next byte
options.d: the CPU runs a user written code and does accordingly

question: In the layered approach of Operating Systems __________

options.a: Bottom Layer(0) is the User interface
options.b: Highest Layer(N) is the User interface

question: How does the Hardware trigger an interrupt?

options.a: Sending signals to CPU through a system bus
options.b: Executing a special program called interrupt program
options.c: Executing a special program called system program
options.d: Executing a special operation called system call

question: Which operation is performed by an interrupt handler?

options.a: Saving the current state of the system
options.b: Loading the interrupt handling code and executing it
options.c: Once done handling, bringing back the system to the original state it was before the interrupt occurred
